summary: Matched the common fighter up-smash action/input code by moving the `ftCo_AttackHi4` functions out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` into a new C translation unit and header. The PR added `ftCo_AttackHi4.c` to `obj_files.mk`, exposed typed prototypes in `ftCo_AttackHi4.h`, updated include users, removed stale declarations from `ft_0877.h`, and renamed the remaining assembly callsite in `ftCo_KneeBend_IASA` from a local label to `ftCo_AttackHi4_CheckInputNoD0`.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementations for `ftCo_AttackHi4_CheckInput`, the formerly local `08C948` helper, `doEnter` at `08CA38`, and the action callbacks `ftCo_AttackHi4_Anim`, `ftCo_AttackHi4_IASA`, `ftCo_AttackHi4_Phys`, and `ftCo_AttackHi4_Coll` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.

Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_AttackHi4.c` with C implementations for up-smash input detection, Ness-specific dispatch via `ftNs_AttackHi4_Enter`, item/light-throw handling via `ft_800957F4(gobj, ftCo_MS_LightThrowHi4)`, normal entry via `Fighter_ChangeMotionState(... ftCo_MS_AttackHi4 ...)`, and standard anim/IASA/phys/coll callbacks.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_AttackHi4.h` with address-commented prototypes for the matched functions, including `ftCo_AttackHi4_CheckInputNoD0` for the `08C948` variant.
Updated `obj_files.mk` so `ftCo_AttackHi4.c.o` is built in the ftCommon object list after `ftCo_AttackS4.c.o`.
Changed the remaining assembly call inside `ftCo_KneeBend_IASA` from `.L_8008C948` to the named symbol `ftCo_AttackHi4_CheckInputNoD0`.

Removed `ftCo_AttackHi4_CheckInput` and `ftCo_AttackS4_CheckInput` declarations from the broad `src/melee/ft/ft_0877.h` header and added more targeted includes of `ftCo_AttackHi4.h` and `ftCo_AttackS4.h` to files that need attack input declarations.
Added prototypes for `ft_800DF30C` and `ft_800DF2D8` to `src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h`, matching their use in the new C implementation.
smart_moves: Split the two similar input checks into `checkLStick` and `checkLStickNoD0`, preserving the assembly distinction: the normal path checks both stick threshold `p_ftCommonData->xCC` and tilt timer `p_ftCommonData->xD0`, while the NoD0 knee-bend path omits the timer check.
Kept the special-case dispatch for Ness explicit with `switch (fp->kind)` and `FTKIND_NESS`, matching the original branch to `ftNs_AttackHi4_Enter` while keeping the common case in `doEnter`.

Preserved item-holding behavior before normal attack entry: if `fp->item_gobj` exists and either `ft_80094E54(fp)` or `ft_800DF30C(fp)` passes, the code enters `ftCo_MS_LightThrowHi4` through `ft_800957F4` and returns true.
Moved declarations out of a catch-all fighter header into the action-specific header and updated direct include users, reducing header pollution and making dependencies more precise.
Renamed an internal assembly label used from still-asm code to `ftCo_AttackHi4_CheckInputNoD0`, improving cross-boundary readability while the surrounding file remains partially unmatched.

Matched simple callbacks by delegating to existing common routines: `ftCo_AttackHi4_Anim` uses `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining` then `ft_8008A2BC`, `Phys` calls `ft_80084F3C`, and `Coll` calls `ft_80084104`.
decomp_lessons: When matching duplicated input routines, name the semantic difference rather than leaving address labels; here `CheckInputNoD0` records that the second variant omits the `p_ftCommonData->xD0` timer comparison.
For fighter common actions, action-specific headers such as `ftCo_AttackHi4.h` are preferable to adding declarations to broad numbered headers like `ft_0877.h`.
A matched C file must be added to `obj_files.mk`; otherwise removing assembly text is not enough for the build to link the replacement object.
Preserve character-specific branches in common action code even when most fighters share the same entry routine; up-smash uses a Ness-specific enter function.
For bitfield-backed fighter state such as `allow_interrupt`, direct semantic fields can replace raw byte/bit operations when the struct field is already known.
Small wrapper callbacks often match cleanly as single-call C functions and should be moved together with their action's input/enter code to keep the action module coherent.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_AttackHi4_*` names are used for common fighter up-smash functions.
`ftCo_AttackHi4_CheckInputNoD0` names the `08C948` variant that skips the `xD0` stick-tilt timer threshold.
`doEnter` is a local/common enter helper for the normal `ftCo_MS_AttackHi4` motion-state transition.
Header guards in this slice use address-based names such as `GALE01_08C830`.
Function prototypes in the new header retain address comments like `/* 08C830 */`, `/* 08C948 */`, and `/* 08CA98 */`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly float constants `.L_804D8490`, `.L_804D8498`, and `.L_804D849C` with C literals/arguments through existing fields and function calls.
Mapped the `x668` input bit test to `fp->input.x668 & HSD_Pad_A`.
Mapped the vertical stick threshold load from `0x624` to `fp->input.lstick.y` and compared it against `p_ftCommonData->xCC`.
Mapped the tilt timer byte at `0x671` to `fp->x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y` and compared it against `p_ftCommonData->xD0` for the normal check only.
Mapped the raw item pointer check at fighter offset `0x1974` to `fp->item_gobj != NULL`.
Mapped the kind comparison against value `8` to `FTKIND_NESS`.
Mapped the motion-state change arguments to `Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, ftCo_MS_AttackHi4, Ft_MF_None, NULL, 0, 1, 0)`.
Preserved the remaining asm caller by exporting the formerly local `08C948` routine as `ftCo_AttackHi4_CheckInputNoD0`.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename `doEnter` to a more specific `ftCo_AttackHi4_Enter`-style symbol.
Search for PRs matching `ftCo_AttackLw4_CheckInput`, which remains in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` immediately after the removed up-smash block.

Check whether `ftCo_AttackS4_CheckInput` was also moved to an action-specific header in a nearby PR, since this slice adds `ftCo_AttackS4.h` includes and removes its broad declaration from `ft_0877.h`.
Search for future struct-field naming improvements around `input.x668`, `input.lstick.y`, and `x671_timer_lstick_tilt_y`.
